seoTitle: How to Set Up Alerts and Notifications in Power BI (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to pin visuals to a Power BI dashboard, set threshold-based data alerts, and route notifications through email or Power Automate.
intro: Manually checking your dashboard for revenue drops or KPI breaches doesn't scale once your business grows. This guide shows how to pin a card or KPI visual to a Power BI Service dashboard, configure a threshold-based alert rule, and control how often you get notified. It also covers linking alerts to Power Automate for automated Teams or email workflows.

steps.heading: Pin your visual to a dashboard
steps.body: Open your report in Power BI Service, hover over the target card or KPI visual, and click the Pin visual icon to add it to a dashboard.

steps.heading: Open the alert manager
steps.body: On your dashboard, hover over the pinned card, click the three-dot menu, and select Manage alerts.

steps.heading: Create a new alert rule
steps.body: Click Add alert rule, toggle it active, and give it a clear descriptive name.

steps.heading: Set your threshold condition
steps.body: Choose Above or Below in the Condition dropdown and enter your target numeric threshold value.

steps.heading: Choose notification frequency and save
steps.body: Set your preferred frequency, such as hourly or daily, check the email notification box if desired, and save the rule to activate monitoring.

faq.question: What visuals support Power BI data alerts?
faq.answer: Alerts work with card visuals, KPI blocks, and gauges that have been pinned to a Power BI Service dashboard.

faq.question: How often can I receive alert notifications?
faq.answer: You can set the frequency to as often as once an hour or scale it back to a single daily digest summary.

faq.question: Can alerts trigger actions outside Power BI?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use the Power Automate link inside the alert rule panel to push notifications into Microsoft Teams or custom email workflows.

faq.question: Do I need to republish my report for alerts to work?
faq.answer: No, alerts are configured directly on the pinned dashboard tile in Power BI Service and don't require republishing the underlying report.
